title: creating overlay failed /run/ibpod/infra-container
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start PC
2.
3.


### Describe the results you received

No root containers are showing up.

So far only workaround is to remove the pod and recreate it.

This creates /run/ibpod/infra-container.

### Describe the results you expected

Root containers should start.

The container is configured in /etc/containers/systemd/bind.container.

[Unit]
Description=bind
After=local-fs.target

[Container]
Image=bind
Volume=bind_db:/var/bind
Volume=bind_conf:/etc/bind
Volume=bind_pki:/etc/pki
PodmanArgs=--pod=dns

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

### podman info output

May 23 16:58:30 bat.localdomain systemd[1]: bind.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=126/n/a

comments.body:
Another (better) workaround (which was not necessary before):

[Unit]
Description=bind
After=local-fs.target

[Service]
ExecStartPre=mkdir -p /run/libpod/infra-container

[Container]
Image=bind
Volume=bind_db:/var/bind
Volume=bind_conf:/etc/bind
Volume=bind_pki:/etc/pki
PodmanArgs=--pod=dns

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target

>  PodmanArgs=--pod=dns

Note is generally recommend to use pod units to manage pods with quadlet.

---

But of course regardless the bug here is that the new pause image less pods are broken after a reboot as the code to recreate the tmp directory after reboot seems broken.
cc @jankaluza

> > PodmanArgs=--pod=dns
> 
> Note is generally recommend to use pod units to manage pods with quadlet.
> 
> But of course regardless the bug here is that the new pause image less pods are broken after a reboot as the code to recreate the tmp directory after reboot seems broken. cc [@jankaluza](https://github.com/jankaluza)

Can you give an example how I should do it? This is the only way I know so far with pods.

An example for a pod quadlet? https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#pod-units-pod
Just need to create a pod unit (i.e. `mytest.pod`) and then in the container unit have `Pod=mytest.pod`

Yeah - this works. 

Important was to put "After=dns.pod" int the Unit section of the container. Otherwise the container is not starting after boot because the pod is still not there.

```
[Unit]
Description=dns
After=local-fs.target

[Pod]
PodName=dns
DNS=::1
DNSSearch=localdoamin
Network=podman1
IP=10.90.0.2
IP6=fdc2:4ba9:85d4:f3c3::2

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

```
[Unit]
Description=bind
After=dns.pod

[Container]
Image=bind
Volume=bind_db:/var/bind
Volume=bind_conf:/etc/bind
Volume=bind_pki:/etc/pki
PodmanArgs=--pod=dns

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

That is not needed, quadlet manages the dependencies but you must use `Pod=dns.pod` in the container unit or however you called the file. `PodmanArgs` bypasses quadlets logic as it just appends the args directly.

Yes - I can confirm it works this way.

```
Unit]
Description=bind

[Container]
Pod=dns.pod
Image=localhost/bind
Volume=bind_db:/var/bind
Volume=bind_conf:/etc/bind
Volume=bind_pki:/etc/pki

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```
